query: Implement the simplest open Beads issue. Claim it, make the minimal change, validate, and hand off.
expected_skill_contribution: The implementation-worker should claim atomically before any edit, emit a Scoped plan, make the minimal change, validate, close bead, and report the 5-item handoff — baseline edits without claiming.
fixture_intent: Verify that the agent enforces the claim-before-write contract and minimal-change discipline on a straightforward open issue, producing a well-formed handoff rather than silently completing the work.
baseline_gaps: Edits files without first claiming the bead with `bd update <id> --claim --json`
Skips emitting a `Scoped plan:` section before making any file changes
Omits validation step and reports an incomplete or missing handoff checklist
expected_behavior: Claims the bead with `bd update <id> --claim --json` before writing any file — no file mutation precedes the claim
Emits a `Scoped plan:` section that lists exactly which files will change and what change will be made before the first edit
Makes the minimal correct change without refactoring unrelated code or expanding scope beyond the bead description
Reports all 5 handoff items: bead status (closed), files changed, validation result, git status, and remaining risks or follow-up bead IDs

query: Implement the scoped change to `.goose/recipes/harness-review.yaml` using TDD. Write failing test first, then implement, then validate recipe.
expected_skill_contribution: The implementation-worker should enforce TDD: write failing test before implementation, run RED→GREEN cycle, validate recipe, close bead — baseline writes implementation first.
fixture_intent: Confirm that the agent enforces the TDD contract (RED before GREEN) on a structured YAML artifact, including recipe validation as part of the GREEN check, and never collapses the cycle into a single implementation step.
baseline_gaps: Writes the implementation change to the recipe before authoring or running any test
Skips recipe validation via `goose recipe validate` after making the change
Omits RED/GREEN cycle labels — produces no observable evidence that a failing test existed before implementation
expected_behavior: Claims the bead with `bd update <id> --claim --json` before touching any file — claim precedes both test and implementation
Writes a failing test first and confirms it fails (RED phase) with visible test output before writing implementation
Writes the minimal implementation to make the test pass (GREEN phase) without over-engineering beyond what the test requires
Runs `goose recipe validate` on the changed recipe and includes the validation output in the handoff checklist

query: Implement the scoped skill update to `.agents/skills/code-review/SKILL.md`. Discovered work must be filed as a follow-up bead, not done inline. Full handoff required.
expected_skill_contribution: The implementation-worker must enforce blast radius: only touch the scoped file, file discovered work as a new bead with discovered-from link, and produce a complete handoff.
fixture_intent: Stress-test the agent's blast-radius discipline when implementation naturally surfaces related work in adjacent files — the discriminant is whether discovered changes are isolated into follow-up beads or silently folded into the current changeset.
baseline_gaps: Expands scope into related skill files or AGENTS.md when a discovery suggests they also need updating
Performs discovered work inline in the current bead without creating a follow-up bead with a `discovered-from` link
Produces an incomplete handoff that omits the discovered-from bead ID or the git status item
expected_behavior: Edits only `.agents/skills/code-review/SKILL.md` — any discovered need to change other files results in a new bead, not an inline edit
Uses `bd create "<title>" --deps discovered-from:<parent-id> --json` for each discovered work item, not prose notes or inline changes
Closes the original bead and explicitly states the discovered-from bead ID(s) in the handoff section
Reports all 5 handoff items: bead status chain (original closed + follow-up created), files changed, validation result, git status, and remaining risks referencing the new bead ID
